title: Implement software inventory and approval provenance

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows

commands.name: Missing software inventory module and routes
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/software-inventory.test.ts --project property tests/property/software-inventory.property.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/software-inventory.test.ts apps/web/tests/integration/software-inventory-routes.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The inventory module and software HTTP route did not exist

commands.name: Undefined software inventory acceptance steps
commands.expectedFailure: Three inventory scenarios and eighteen steps were undefined

commands.name: Focused software inventory contracts
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/software-inventory.test.ts tests/unit/domain-schemas.test.ts --project property tests/property/software-inventory.property.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/software-inventory.test.ts

commands.name: Production build and focused visual capture
commands.command: pnpm --filter @pactwire/web build && PACTWIRE_CAPTURE_CURATED_EVIDENCE=1 PACTWIRE_EVIDENCE_TASK=AUT-02 PACTWIRE_BDD_PRODUCTION=1 pnpm test:bdd

commands.name: Expanded regression exposed evidence and parallel-startup gaps
commands.command: pnpm test:unit && pnpm test:property && pnpm test:integration
commands.expectedFailure: The first expanded run rejected the missing AUT-02 manifest and the five-second integration timeout under parallel PGlite startup; both gaps were fixed before the full green gate

scenarios.scenario: A privacy officer imports an existing district approval
A test operator can read inventory but cannot set an imported status
Approval-state filtering preserves the source label

assets.altText: Desktop software inventory showing a fictional approved district record with its imported source and explicit no-run state.
assets.caption: The imported APPROVED status names the fictional district registry and is labelled as a district record, not a Pactwire conclusion.

assets.altText: Narrow software inventory filtered to approved records with readable district source, empty states, and next action.
assets.caption: The APPROVED filter preserves source provenance and bounded no-run language at a 390-pixel viewport.

proofs.description: Approval-origin, tenant URL, source copy, and bounded empty-state examples
PostgreSQL transactions, immutability, filters, and tenant isolation
Signed-session, server-role, invalid-origin, filter, and cross-workspace HTTP checks
Imported source, operator denial, and approval-filter browser journeys
Playwright inventory steps and strict browser failure gate
Inventory service, summaries, and in-memory/PostgreSQL repositories
Inventory detail and immutable approval-origin persistence
Curated AUT-02 red/green record and bounded limitations

knownLimitations: Agreement, authorization, run, and finding summaries remain explicit empty states until their owning tasks are implemented.
The browser fixture uses in-memory storage while PostgreSQL persistence is verified in integration tests.
Raw GitHub Actions artifacts follow the repository retention policy; the curated, source-bound screenshots remain in this repository.
